Example

Class ExternalPlanSetup

Namespace
VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API
Assembly
VMS.TPS.Common.Model.API.dll

Represents an external beam plan. For more information, see the definition of the DICOM RT Plan.

public class ExternalPlanSetup : PlanSetup, IXmlSerializable, IDVHEstimationCalculator
Inheritance
object
SerializableObject
ApiDataObject
PlanningItem
PlanSetup
ExternalPlanSetup
Implements
IXmlSerializable
IDVHEstimationCalculator
Inherited Members
PlanSetup.m_errorsOnCalculationCompleted
PlanSetup.GetProtocolPrescriptionsAndMeasures(ref List<ProtocolPhasePrescription>, ref List<ProtocolPhaseMeasure>)

PlanSetup.DetachFromCalcClient(ICalculationClient)

Properties

DoseAsEvaluationDose

The evaluation dose is connected to the plan and contains voxels that are set by SetVoxels instead of the dose calculation algorithm. Returns null if the plan has no evaluation dose.

public EvaluationDose DoseAsEvaluationDose { get; }

Property Value

EvaluationDose

TradeoffExplorationContext

[Availability of this property depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Gets the TradeoffExplorationContext.

public TradeoffExplorationContext TradeoffExplorationContext { get; }

Property Value

TradeoffExplorationContext

Remarks

To be able to get the TradeoffExplorationContext class, the plan needs to have the optimization objectives set.

Methods

AddArcBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters, VRect<double>, double, double, double, GantryDirection, double, VVector)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Adds a new arc beam to the plan.

public Beam AddArcBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters machineParameters, VRect<double> jawPositions, double collimatorAngle, double gantryAngle, double gantryStop, GantryDirection gantryDirection, double patientSupportAngle, VVector isocenter)

Parameters

machineParameters ExternalBeamMachineParameters

Configuration of the used treatment unit. See ExternalBeamMachineParameters.

jawPositions VRect<double>

The jaw positions of the treatment beam in millimeters, and in IEC BEAM LIMITING DEVICE coordinates.

collimatorAngle double

The orientation of the IEC BEAM LIMITING DEVICE coordinate system with respect to the IEC GANTRY coordinate system (in degrees).

gantryAngle double

The orientation of the IEC GANTRY coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

gantryStop double

The stop angle of the gantry, in degrees.

gantryDirection GantryDirection

The rotation direction of the arc.

patientSupportAngle double

The orientation of the IEC PATIENT SUPPORT (turntable) coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

isocenter VVector

The isocenter position, in millimeters.

Returns

Beam

The created Beam.

Remarks

All angles must be specified in the range [0, 360[.

AddConformalArcBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters, double, int, double, double, GantryDirection, double, VVector)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Adds a new conformal arc beam to the plan. Halcyon machines are not supported.

public Beam AddConformalArcBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters machineParameters, double collimatorAngle, int controlPointCount, double gantryAngle, double gantryStop, GantryDirection gantryDirection, double patientSupportAngle, VVector isocenter)

Parameters

machineParameters ExternalBeamMachineParameters

Configuration of the used treatment unit. See ExternalBeamMachineParameters.

collimatorAngle double

The orientation of the IEC BEAM LIMITING DEVICE coordinate system with respect to the IEC GANTRY coordinate system (in degrees).

controlPointCount int

The number of control points to use.

gantryAngle double

The orientation of the IEC GANTRY coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

gantryStop double

The stop angle of the gantry, in degrees.

gantryDirection GantryDirection

The rotation direction of the arc.

patientSupportAngle double

The orientation of the IEC PATIENT SUPPORT (turntable) coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

isocenter VVector

The isocenter position, in millimeters.

Returns

Beam

The created beam.

Remarks

All angles must be specified in the range [0, 360[.

AddFixedSequenceBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters, double, double, VVector)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Adds a new fixed sequence (flattening sequence) beam to the plan.

public Beam AddFixedSequenceBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters machineParameters, double collimatorAngle, double gantryAngle, VVector isocenter)

Parameters

machineParameters ExternalBeamMachineParameters

Configuration of the used treatment unit. See ExternalBeamMachineParameters. The machine must be Halcyon.

collimatorAngle double

The orientation of the IEC BEAM LIMITING DEVICE coordinate system with respect to the IEC GANTRY coordinate system (in degrees).

gantryAngle double

The orientation of the IEC GANTRY coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

isocenter VVector

The isocenter position, in millimeters.

Returns

Beam

The created beam.

AddImagingSetup(ExternalBeamMachineParameters, ImagingBeamSetupParameters, Structure)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Adds new imaging beam or beams to the plan. First removes the existing imaging beams. If MV imaging beams are involved, the plan should be optimized again.

public bool AddImagingSetup(ExternalBeamMachineParameters machineParameters, ImagingBeamSetupParameters setupParameters, Structure targetStructure)

Parameters

machineParameters ExternalBeamMachineParameters

Configuration of the used treatment unit. See ExternalBeamMachineParameters.

setupParameters ImagingBeamSetupParameters

Setup parameters for the imaging beam. See ImagingBeamSetupParameters.

targetStructure Structure

Target structure for imaging. If not specified, the target structure in the plan is used.

Returns

bool

True if imaging beams were successfully created.

AddMLCArcBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters, float[,], VRect<double>, double, double, double, GantryDirection, double, VVector)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Adds a new arc beam with an MLC to the plan.

public Beam AddMLCArcBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters machineParameters, float[,] leafPositions, VRect<double> jawPositions, double collimatorAngle, double gantryAngle, double gantryStop, GantryDirection gantryDirection, double patientSupportAngle, VVector isocenter)

Parameters

machineParameters ExternalBeamMachineParameters

Configuration of the used treatment unit. See ExternalBeamMachineParameters.

leafPositions float[,]

The positions of the beam collimator leaf pairs in millimeters, and in the IEC BEAMLIMITING DEVICE coordinate axis appropriate to the MLC device type. For example, the X-axis for MLCX and the Y-axis for MLCY. The two-dimensional array is indexed [bank, leaf], where the bank is either 0 or 1. Bank 0 represents the leaf bank to the negative MLC X direction, and bank 1 to the positive MLC X direction. Passing null selects the default.

jawPositions VRect<double>

The jaw positions of the treatment beam in millimeters, and in IEC BEAM LIMITING DEVICE coordinates.

collimatorAngle double

The orientation of the IEC BEAM LIMITING DEVICE coordinate system with respect to the IEC GANTRY coordinate system (in degrees).

gantryAngle double

The orientation of the IEC GANTRY coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

gantryStop double

The stop angle of the gantry, in degrees.

gantryDirection GantryDirection

The direction of the arc.

patientSupportAngle double

The orientation of the IEC PATIENT SUPPORT (turntable) coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

isocenter VVector

The isocenter position, in millimeters.

Returns

Beam

The created beam.

Remarks

All angles must be specified in the range [0, 360[.

AddMLCBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters, float[,], VRect<double>, double, double, double, VVector)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Adds a new beam with an MLC to the plan.

public Beam AddMLCBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters machineParameters, float[,] leafPositions, VRect<double> jawPositions, double collimatorAngle, double gantryAngle, double patientSupportAngle, VVector isocenter)

Parameters

machineParameters ExternalBeamMachineParameters

Configuration of the used treatment unit. See ExternalBeamMachineParameters.

leafPositions float[,]

The positions of the beam collimator leaf pairs in millimeters, and in the IEC BEAMLIMITING DEVICE coordinate axis appropriate to the MLC device type. For example, the X-axis for MLCX and the Y-axis for MLCY. The two-dimensional array is indexed [bank, leaf] where the bank is either 0 or 1. Bank 0 represents the leaf bank to the negative MLC X direction, and bank 1 to the positive MLC X direction. Passing null selects the default.

jawPositions VRect<double>

The jaw positions of the treatment beam in millimeters, and in IEC BEAM LIMITING DEVICE coordinates.

collimatorAngle double

The orientation of the IEC BEAM LIMITING DEVICE coordinate system with respect to the IEC GANTRY coordinate system (in degrees).

gantryAngle double

The orientation of the IEC GANTRY coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

patientSupportAngle double

The orientation of the IEC PATIENT SUPPORT (turntable) coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

isocenter VVector

The isocenter position, in millimeters.

Returns

Beam

The created beam.

Remarks

All angles must be specified in the range [0, 360[.

AddMLCSetupBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters, float[,], VRect<double>, double, double, double, VVector)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Adds a new static setup field with an MLC to the plan.

public Beam AddMLCSetupBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters machineParameters, float[,] leafPositions, VRect<double> jawPositions, double collimatorAngle, double gantryAngle, double patientSupportAngle, VVector isocenter)

Parameters

machineParameters ExternalBeamMachineParameters

Configuration of the used treatment unit. See ExternalBeamMachineParameters.

leafPositions float[,]

The positions of the beam collimator leaf pairs in millimeters, and in the IEC BEAMLIMITING DEVICE coordinate axis appropriate to the MLC device type. For example, the X-axis for MLCX and the Y-axis for MLCY. The two-dimensional array is indexed [bank, leaf] where the bank is either 0 or 1. Bank 0 represents the leaf bank to the negative MLC X direction, and bank 1 to the positive MLC X direction. Passing null selects the default.

jawPositions VRect<double>

The jaw positions of the setup field in millimeters, and in IEC BEAM LIMITING DEVICE coordinates.

collimatorAngle double

The orientation of the IEC BEAM LIMITING DEVICE coordinate system with respect to the IEC GANTRY coordinate system (in degrees).

gantryAngle double

The orientation of the IEC GANTRY coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

patientSupportAngle double

The orientation of the IEC PATIENT SUPPORT (turntable) coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

isocenter VVector

The isocenter position, in millimeters.

Returns

Beam

The created beam.

Remarks

All angles must be specified in the range [0, 360[.

AddMultipleStaticSegmentBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters, IEnumerable<double>, double, double, double, VVector)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Adds a new IMRT beam to the plan and defines Multiple Static Segments as the delivery technique.

public Beam AddMultipleStaticSegmentBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters machineParameters, IEnumerable<double> metersetWeights, double collimatorAngle, double gantryAngle, double patientSupportAngle, VVector isocenter)

Parameters

machineParameters ExternalBeamMachineParameters

Configuration of the used treatment unit. See ExternalBeamMachineParameters.

metersetWeights IEnumerable<double>

The meterset weight for each control point of the beam. The number of meterset weight items defines the number of created control points.

collimatorAngle double

The orientation of the IEC BEAM LIMITING DEVICE coordinate system with respect to the IEC GANTRY coordinate system (in degrees).

gantryAngle double

The orientation of the IEC GANTRY coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

patientSupportAngle double

The orientation of the IEC PATIENT SUPPORT (turntable) coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

isocenter VVector

The isocenter position, in millimeters.

Returns

Beam

The created beam.

Remarks

All angles must be specified in the range [0, 360[.

AddSetupBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters, VRect<double>, double, double, double, VVector)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Adds a new static setup field to the plan.

public Beam AddSetupBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters machineParameters, VRect<double> jawPositions, double collimatorAngle, double gantryAngle, double patientSupportAngle, VVector isocenter)

Parameters

machineParameters ExternalBeamMachineParameters

Configuration of the used treatment unit. See ExternalBeamMachineParameters.

jawPositions VRect<double>

The jaw positions of the setup field in millimeters, and in IEC BEAM LIMITING DEVICE coordinates.

collimatorAngle double

The orientation of the IEC BEAM LIMITING DEVICE coordinate system with respect to the IEC GANTRY coordinate system (in degrees).

gantryAngle double

The orientation of the IEC GANTRY coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

patientSupportAngle double

The orientation of the IEC PATIENT SUPPORT (turntable) coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

isocenter VVector

The isocenter position, in millimeters.

Returns

Beam

The created beam.

Remarks

All angles must be specified in the range [0, 360[.

AddSlidingWindowBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters, IEnumerable<double>, double, double, double, VVector)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Adds a new IMRT beam to the plan and defines Sliding Window as the delivery technique.

public Beam AddSlidingWindowBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters machineParameters, IEnumerable<double> metersetWeights, double collimatorAngle, double gantryAngle, double patientSupportAngle, VVector isocenter)

Parameters

machineParameters ExternalBeamMachineParameters

Configuration of the used treatment unit. See ExternalBeamMachineParameters.

metersetWeights IEnumerable<double>

The meterset weight for each control point of the beam. The number of meterset weight items defines the number of created control points.

collimatorAngle double

The orientation of the IEC BEAM LIMITING DEVICE coordinate system with respect to the IEC GANTRY coordinate system (in degrees).

gantryAngle double

The orientation of the IEC GANTRY coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

patientSupportAngle double

The orientation of the IEC PATIENT SUPPORT (turntable) coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

isocenter VVector

The isocenter position, in millimeters.

Returns

Beam

The created beam.

Remarks

All angles must be specified in the range [0, 360[.

AddSlidingWindowBeamForFixedJaws(ExternalBeamMachineParameters, IEnumerable<double>, double, double, double, VVector)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Adds a new sliding window IMRT beam and takes jaw positions from machine configuration. In case of a Halcyon machine, the special leaf position requirements are taken into account.

public Beam AddSlidingWindowBeamForFixedJaws(ExternalBeamMachineParameters machineParameters, IEnumerable<double> metersetWeights, double collimatorAngle, double gantryAngle, double patientSupportAngle, VVector isocenter)

Parameters

machineParameters ExternalBeamMachineParameters

Configuration of the used treatment unit. See ExternalBeamMachineParameters.

metersetWeights IEnumerable<double>

The meterset weight for each control point of the beam. The number of meterset weight items defines the number of created control points.

collimatorAngle double

The orientation of the IEC BEAM LIMITING DEVICE coordinate system with respect to the IEC GANTRY coordinate system (in degrees).

gantryAngle double

Gantry angle in the IEC GANTRY coordinate system (in degrees).

patientSupportAngle double

The orientation of the IEC PATIENT SUPPORT (turntable) coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

isocenter VVector

The isocenter position in millimeters.

Returns

Beam

The created beam.

Remarks

All angles must be specified in the range [0, 360[.

AddStaticBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters, VRect<double>, double, double, double, VVector)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Adds a new static beam to the plan.

public Beam AddStaticBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters machineParameters, VRect<double> jawPositions, double collimatorAngle, double gantryAngle, double patientSupportAngle, VVector isocenter)

Parameters

machineParameters ExternalBeamMachineParameters

Configuration of the used treatment unit. See ExternalBeamMachineParameters.

jawPositions VRect<double>

The jaw positions of the treatment beam in millimeters, and in IEC BEAM LIMITING DEVICE coordinates.

collimatorAngle double

The orientation of the IEC BEAM LIMITING DEVICE coordinate system with respect to the IEC GANTRY coordinate system (in degrees).

gantryAngle double

The orientation of the IEC GANTRY coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

patientSupportAngle double

The orientation of the IEC PATIENT SUPPORT (turntable) coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

isocenter VVector

The isocenter position, in millimeters.

Returns

Beam

The created beam.

Remarks

All angles must be specified in the range [0, 360[.

AddVMATBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters, IEnumerable<double>, double, double, double, GantryDirection, double, VVector)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Adds a new VMAT beam to the plan.

public Beam AddVMATBeam(ExternalBeamMachineParameters machineParameters, IEnumerable<double> metersetWeights, double collimatorAngle, double gantryAngle, double gantryStop, GantryDirection gantryDirection, double patientSupportAngle, VVector isocenter)

Parameters

machineParameters ExternalBeamMachineParameters

Configuration of the used treatment unit. See ExternalBeamMachineParameters.

metersetWeights IEnumerable<double>

The meterset weight for each control point of the beam. The number of meterset weight items defines the number of created control points.

collimatorAngle double

The orientation of the IEC BEAM LIMITING DEVICE coordinate system with respect to the IEC GANTRY coordinate system (in degrees).

gantryAngle double

The orientation of the IEC GANTRY coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

gantryStop double

The stop angle of the gantry, in degrees.

gantryDirection GantryDirection

The rotation direction of the arc.

patientSupportAngle double

The orientation of the IEC PATIENT SUPPORT (turntable) coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

isocenter VVector

The isocenter position, in millimeters.

Returns

Beam

The created beam.

Remarks

All angles must be specified in the range [0, 360[.

AddVMATBeamForFixedJaws(ExternalBeamMachineParameters, IEnumerable<double>, double, double, double, GantryDirection, double, VVector)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Adds a new VMAT beam and takes jaw positions from machine configuration. In case of a Halcyon machine, the special leaf position requirements are taken into account.

public Beam AddVMATBeamForFixedJaws(ExternalBeamMachineParameters machineParameters, IEnumerable<double> metersetWeights, double collimatorAngle, double gantryStartAngle, double gantryStopAngle, GantryDirection gantryDir, double patientSupportAngle, VVector isocenter)

Parameters

machineParameters ExternalBeamMachineParameters

Configuration of the used treatment unit. See ExternalBeamMachineParameters.

metersetWeights IEnumerable<double>

The meterset weight for each control point of the beam. The number of meterset weight items defines the number of created control points.

collimatorAngle double

The orientation of the IEC BEAM LIMITING DEVICE coordinate system with respect to the IEC GANTRY coordinate system (in degrees).

gantryStartAngle double

Gantry start angle in the IEC GANTRY coordinate system (in degrees).

gantryStopAngle double

Gantry stop angle in the IEC GANTRY coordinate system (in degrees).

gantryDir GantryDirection

Gantry direction

patientSupportAngle double

The orientation of the IEC PATIENT SUPPORT (turntable) coordinate system with respect to the IEC FIXED REFERENCE coordinate system (in degrees).

isocenter VVector

The isocenter position in millimeters.

Returns

Beam

The created beam.

Remarks

All angles must be specified in the range [0, 360[.

CalculateDVHEstimates(string, Dictionary<string, DoseValue>, Dictionary<string, string>)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Calculates DVH estimates for the plan.

public CalculationResult CalculateDVHEstimates(string modelId, Dictionary<string, DoseValue> targetDoseLevels, Dictionary<string, string> structureMatches)

Parameters

modelId string

DVH estimation model ID.

targetDoseLevels Dictionary<string, DoseValue>

Dose levels for the target structures.

structureMatches Dictionary<string, string>

Matching between the IDs of the structures contained in the structure set used in the plan (the Key in the Dictionary) and the structures used in the DVH estimation model (the Value in the Dictionary).

Returns

CalculationResult

The calculation result. See the calculation details in the calculation logs

CalculateDose()

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Calculates the dose for the plan.

public CalculationResult CalculateDose()

Returns

CalculationResult

The calculation result. See calculation details from the calculation logs.

CalculateDoseWithPresetValues(List<KeyValuePair<string, MetersetValue>>)

Calculates the dose for the plan with preset meterset values. This applies only to IMRT beams.

public CalculationResult CalculateDoseWithPresetValues(List<KeyValuePair<string, MetersetValue>> presetValues)

Parameters

presetValues List<KeyValuePair<string, MetersetValue>>

The list of preset meterset values for the beams. An element of the list should contain a pair consisting of the beam identifier (as the key) and the corresponding preset meterset value for the beam. Each beam in the plan must have one entry in the list.

Returns

CalculationResult

The calculation result. See calculation details from the calculation logs.

CalculateLeafMotions()

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Calculates leaf motions using the calculation options of the plan setup. Before calling this method, set the calculation models for leaf motions and dose calculation.

public CalculationResult CalculateLeafMotions()

Returns

CalculationResult

The calculation result. See calculation details from the calculation logs.

See Also
SetCalculationModel(CalculationType, string)
SetCalculationOption(string, string, string)

CalculateLeafMotions(LMCMSSOptions)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Calculates leaf motions using the given calculation options, which override the calculation options defined for the plan setup. Before calling this method, set the calculation models for leaf motions and dose calculation. The used leaf motion calculation algorithm must be MSS Leaf Motion Calculator (LMCMSS).

public CalculationResult CalculateLeafMotions(LMCMSSOptions options)

Parameters

options LMCMSSOptions

Calculation options for the MSS Leaf Motion Calculator (LMCMSS) algorithm.

Returns

CalculationResult

The calculation result. See calculation details from the calculation logs.

See Also
SetCalculationModel(CalculationType, string)
SetCalculationOption(string, string, string)

CalculateLeafMotions(LMCVOptions)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Calculates leaf motions using the given calculation options, which override the calculation options defined for the plan setup. Before calling this method, set the calculation models for leaf motions and dose calculation. The used leaf motion calculation algorithm must be Varian Leaf Motion Calculator (LMCV).

public CalculationResult CalculateLeafMotions(LMCVOptions options)

Parameters

options LMCVOptions

Calculation options for the Varian Leaf Motion Calculator (LMCV) algorithm.

Returns

CalculationResult

The calculation result. See calculation details from the calculation logs.

See Also
SetCalculationModel(CalculationType, string)
SetCalculationOption(string, string, string)

CalculateLeafMotions(SmartLMCOptions)

Calculates leaf motions using the given calculation options, which override the calculation options defined for the plan setup. Before calling this method, set the calculation models for leaf motions and dose calculation. The used leaf motion calculation algorithm must be Varian Smart LMC.

public CalculationResult CalculateLeafMotions(SmartLMCOptions options)

Parameters

options SmartLMCOptions

Calculation options for the Varian Smart LMC algorithm.

Returns

CalculationResult

The calculation result. See calculation details from the calculation logs.

See Also
SetCalculationModel(CalculationType, string)
SetCalculationOption(string, string, string)

CalculateLeafMotionsAndDose()

Calculate leaf motions and dose using the calculation models defined in the plan setup.

public CalculationResult CalculateLeafMotionsAndDose()

Returns

CalculationResult

Result of the intermediate dose calculation.

CalculatePlanUncertaintyDoses()

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Calculates the plan uncertainty dose for the photon plan.

public CalculationResult CalculatePlanUncertaintyDoses()

Returns

CalculationResult

The calculation result. See calculation details in the calculation logs.

CopyEvaluationDose(Dose)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Creates an evaluation dose for the plan. The voxels in an evaluation dose are copied from the existing dose matrix. To create an evaluation dose, the plan must not contain any beams.

public EvaluationDose CopyEvaluationDose(Dose existing)

Parameters

existing Dose

The dose matrix is copied from this object.

Returns

EvaluationDose

A new evaluation dose object.

CreateEvaluationDose()

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Creates an evaluation dose for the plan. The voxels in an evaluation dose can be set using the Eclipse Scripting API instead of a dose calculation algorithm. To create an evaluation dose, the plan must not contain any beams. To set the evaluation dose voxels, retrieve the dose matrix using the DoseAsEvaluationDose property of the plan and then use SetVoxels.

public EvaluationDose CreateEvaluationDose()

Returns

EvaluationDose

A new evaluation dose object.

Remarks

Saving modifications to the database is not possible if the evaluation dose has been created but voxels have not been set.

GetModelsForCalculationType(CalculationType)

Returns a list of calculation models for the given calculation type.

public IEnumerable<string> GetModelsForCalculationType(CalculationType calculationType)

Parameters

calculationType CalculationType

The type of calculation.

Returns

IEnumerable<string>

The list of calculation models.

Optimize()

Runs IMRT optimization for the plan setup. The Multileaf Collimator (MLC) is determined automatically. If there are more than one MLC or no MLC at all, an exception is thrown. Plan normalization method is changed to 'No plan normalization' after successful optimization.

public OptimizerResult Optimize()

Returns

OptimizerResult

The result of the optimization.

Optimize(int)

Runs IMRT optimization for the plan setup. All existing optimal fluences are removed from the beams. Fails if the method cannot select an MLC for the beams. Plan normalization method is changed to 'No plan normalization' after successful optimization.

public OptimizerResult Optimize(int maxIterations)

Parameters

maxIterations int

The maximum number of iterations.

Returns

OptimizerResult

The result of the optimization.

Optimize(int, OptimizationOption)

Runs IMRT optimization for the plan setup. Fails if the method cannot select an MLC for the beams. Plan normalization method is changed to 'No plan normalization' after successful optimization.

public OptimizerResult Optimize(int maxIterations, OptimizationOption optimizationOption)

Parameters

maxIterations int

The maximum number of iterations.

optimizationOption OptimizationOption

Optimization options.

Returns

OptimizerResult

The result of the optimization.

Optimize(int, OptimizationOption, string)

Runs IMRT optimization for the plan setup. You can add the Multileaf Collimator (MLC) as a parameter. Plan normalization method is changed to 'No plan normalization' after successful optimization.

public OptimizerResult Optimize(int maxIterations, OptimizationOption optimizationOption, string mlcId)

Parameters

maxIterations int

The maximum number of iterations.

optimizationOption OptimizationOption

Optimization option specifying the initial state.

mlcId string

The identifier for the Multileaf Collimator (MLC).

Returns

OptimizerResult

The result of the optimization.

Optimize(OptimizationOptionsIMRT)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Runs IMRT optimization for the plan setup. Plan normalization method is changed to 'No plan normalization' after successful optimization.

public OptimizerResult Optimize(OptimizationOptionsIMRT options)

Parameters

options OptimizationOptionsIMRT

Options specifying initial state, intermediate dose, and termination upon convergence.

Returns

OptimizerResult

Result of the optimization.

OptimizeVMAT()

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Runs VMAT optimization for the plan setup. The Multileaf Collimator (MLC) is determined automatically. If there are more than one MLC or no MLC at all, an exception is thrown. Plan normalization method is changed to 'No plan normalization' after successful optimization.

public OptimizerResult OptimizeVMAT()

Returns

OptimizerResult

The result of the optimization.

OptimizeVMAT(string)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Runs VMAT optimization for the plan setup. You can add the MLC as a parameter. Plan normalization method is changed to 'No plan normalization' after successful optimization.

public OptimizerResult OptimizeVMAT(string mlcId)

Parameters

mlcId string

The identifier for the Multileaf Collimator (MLC).

Returns

OptimizerResult

The result of the optimization.

OptimizeVMAT(OptimizationOptionsVMAT)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Runs VMAT optimization for the plan setup using intermediate dose calculation. Plan normalization method is changed to 'No plan normalization' after successful optimization.

public OptimizerResult OptimizeVMAT(OptimizationOptionsVMAT options)

Parameters

options OptimizationOptionsVMAT

VMAT optimization options.

Returns

OptimizerResult

The result of the optimization.

RemoveBeam(Beam)

[Availability of this method depends on your Eclipse Scripting API license] Removes a beam from the plan.

public void RemoveBeam(Beam beam)

Parameters

beam Beam

The beam that will be removed from the plan.

WriteXml(XmlWriter)

Converts an object into its XML representation. For more information on how to serialize an object into an XML document, see System.Xml.Serialization.XmlSerializer.

public override void WriteXml(XmlWriter writer)

Parameters

writer XmlWriter

The System.Xml.XmlWriter stream, to which the object is serialized.

Made with docfx
